data.title: Amazon expands multi-channel fulfillment to Shein, Shopify, and Walmart third party merchants
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) will now offer third-party merchants the ability to fulfill orders from other sales channels including Walmart (WMT), SHEIN, and Shopify (SHOP) through Amazon’s (NASDAQ:AMZN) Multi-Channel Fulfillment (MCF).

Amazon’s MCF gives merchants the option to have Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) pick, pack, and ship their orders from channels beyond Amazon.com, enabling merchants to give their customers the same experience as if ordered directly from Amazon.

This expansion to Walmart (WMT), SHEIN, and Shopify (SHOP) is part of the company’s focus on helping merchants build and scale their businesses on Amazon.com, as well as on other sales channels. According to Amazon (AMZN), merchants have reported an average 19% increase in sales, as well as reduced out-of-stock rates and improved inventory turnover from one shared pool of inventory.

By acting as a third-party logistics provider, Amazon (AMZN) generates revenue through its MCF with monthly storage fees, along with pick, packing, and shipping fees. The latter of which vary based on the size and weight of the merchandise. For merchants that sell products directly from the Amazon marketplace, the company charges a 15% or higher referral fee.   

By the end of the year, Amazon (AMZN) will make it easier for SHEIN merchants to choose Amazon MCF to fulfill marketplace orders through a free app in Amazon Seller Central, Amazon Supply Chain Portal, and the SHEIN seller hub.

Amazon MCF is now available for merchants to fulfill orders from Walmart (WMT) and Shopify (SHOP).

Source: Press Release

data.title: DexCom falls after short report from Hunterbrook
data.description:
• DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM) dropped 3% after a short report from Hunterbrook Capital.
  • Hunterbrook Capital disclosed that it's short shares of DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM).
  • DexCom didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

data.title: GMTech in talks with WebKey Group for Web3 mobile collaboration
data.description:
• GMTech (OTCPK:GMTH) on Thursday said it is in discussions with WebKey Group Corporation about a potential collaboration in the Web3 mobile ecosystem.
  • The companies are exploring joint efforts in digital asset management, blockchain integration, and on-chain financial infrastructure.
  • WebKey, which focuses on Web3 smartphones and blockchain-based digital solutions, could complement GMTech’s strategy to develop smart hardware and digital finance solutions.
  • The talks are in early stages, and no agreements have been finalized.
  • GMTH is -21.0% to $0.079.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: First Financial declares $0.51 dividend
data.description:
• First Financial (NASDAQ:THFF) declared $0.51/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.52%
  • Payable Oct. 15; for shareholders of record Oct. 1; ex-div Oct. 1.
  • See THFF Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Jefferies gains on report SMFG considering raising stake to about 20%
data.description:
Jefferies Financial Group (NYSE:JEF) rose 3.2% after a report that Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group (SMFG) is evaluating raising its stake in the US investment bank. 

Sumitomo Mitsui is in talks to boost its ~15% stake in Jefferies, possibly by purchasing preferred stock, according to a  Bloomberg report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.  One option the Japanese bank is considering is investing more than 100 billion yen ($677 million) to increase its stake to about 20%, according to the report. 

The two companies are also talking about combining their equities businesses in Japan, possibly through the establishment of a joint venture between SMBC Nikko Securities Inc. and Jefferies’ local operation.

Sumitomo Mitsui was not immediately available for comment to Bloomberg and Jefferies declined to comment,

data.title: Tradeweb Markets downgraded at Rothschild & Co Redburn
data.description:
Financial exchange Tradeweb Markets (NASDAQ:TW) was downgraded at Rothschild & Co Redburn on a lesser growth outlook for the U.S. credit and government bond markets.

Shares were -0.15% Thursday pre-market to $117.03.

"Investor concerns over market share and competitive dynamics in U.S. credit and government bond markets have been gaining momentum. We analyse the complexities of these markets and conclude that former structural tailwinds are diminishing, eroding near-term upside optionality to growth," said analyst Simon Clinch.

"We downgrade Tradeweb to Neutral as its valuation is yet to fully reflect this lesser growth outlook," added Clinch in a research note.

"We expect the company's growth over the next five years, while strong, to be lower than what investors have come to expect. While the valuation has partially derated, there is less upside optionality and we lower our target price from $157 to $129," said the note.

Seeking Alpha authors as well as the sell-side analysts grade the stock as Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system sees the stock as Sell.

data.title: Kilroy Realty acquires Maple Plaza for $205M
data.description:
• Kilroy Realty (NYSE:KRC) announced on Thursday that it has acquired Maple Plaza, a 293,000-square-foot class A office campus in the iconic Beverly Hills submarket of Los Angeles, for $205 million.
  • The acquisition was funded with cash on hand and recent disposition proceeds, marking Kilroy’s first acquisition in Beverly Hills.
  • Maple Plaza is approximately 75% leased to a diverse mix of tenants across private equity, professional services, education, and entertainment, and has experienced strong leasing activity, with 45,000 square feet signed over the past 12 months, said the company. 
  • KRC +1.28% after hours to $44.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hassett backs Fed's quarter-point rate cut, calling it 'a good first step'
data.description:
White House if National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett cheered the Federal Reserve's decision to cut its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points, calling the move "a good first step in the right direction to much lower rates.”

In an interview with CNBC, Hassett, who is among the names on President Donald Trump’s shortlist for the next Fed chair, noted that new Fed Governor Stephen Miran sought a more aggressive 50-bp reduction. Miran's push for a half-point cut was outvoted 11-1 on the rate-setting Federal Open Market Committee.

Miran's assessment was "heartfelt — it’s not dependent on any partisan influence from the White House," the NEC chief said, adding Miran is going to be "an independent Fed governor." Trump, who nominated Miran, has been calling for the Fed to slash borrowing costs for months.

Hassett, speaking a day after the FOMC cut the policy rate for the first time this year, is backing a "prudent" approach to policy decisions.

He specifically said the Fed should "be looking at all the models, to have a diversity of opinions and to decide, what are we going to do in this economy that really looks to be taking off with inflation that’s decelerating but higher than target."

Hassett was on a tear last week about Fed independence.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Bullish sentiment rebounds sharply after three consecutive weekly declines, shows latest AAII data
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors surged after falling for three consecutive weeks, according to the latest survey released by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment as per the weekly AAII investor sentiment survey rose to 41.7% for the week ending September 17, up sharply from 28.0% in the prior update. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment declined to 42.4%, compared to 49.5% the week before.

Investors grew more confident this week amid steady inflation reports and positive developments in the tech sector, including a major chip deal between Intel and Nvidia. Central banks in Europe and the UK kept interest rates steady, which helped ease concerns about a potential recession.

While the Federal Reserve’s 25 basis points rate cut was announced only at the end of the week, it reinforced growing optimism heading into the new week.

Neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also dropped, coming in at 16.0% versus 22.5% the previous week. The decline suggests more investors are moving off the sidelines and forming clearer directional views on the market.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Rithm Capital launches preferred stock offering
data.description:
• Rithm Capital (NYSE:RITM) has launched a public offering of Series E preferred stock and plans to list it on the NYSE under the symbol “RITM PR E.”
  • Underwriters will have a 30-day option to buy up to 15% more shares. Proceeds will be used for investments and general corporate purposes.
  • RITM +0.64% premarket.
